meta.title: Steps
meta.description: A progress indicator with built-in navigation that visually communicates the user's position within a multi-step process such as forms, wizards, and onboarding flows.

mdx:
## Overview

The Steps component helps users understand where they are in a multi-step
process. It provides clear visual feedback about completed, current, and
upcoming steps, making complex workflows feel more manageable and predictable.

### Key Features

- **Three size variants**: Extra small (xs), small (sm), and medium (md) to fit
  different interface densities
- **Two orientations**: Horizontal for wide layouts and vertical for sidebar or
  narrow contexts
- **Flexible indicators**: Numeric step numbers with optional checkmarks, or
  custom icons for semantic meaning
- **Built-in navigation**: Includes navigation triggers and content panels with
  automatic state management

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=7347-81826)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Size

Three sizes are available to match your interface density.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="800">
    <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
      <Text fontWeight="600" fontSize="350">Extra Small (xs) - 24px indicator</Text>
      <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3} size="xs">
        <Steps.List>
          <Steps.Item index={0}>
            <Steps.Trigger>
              <Steps.Indicator>
                <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
              </Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Title>Account</Steps.Title>
            </Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Separator />
          </Steps.Item>
          <Steps.Item index={1}>
            <Steps.Trigger>
              <Steps.Indicator>
                <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
              </Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Title>Profile</Steps.Title>
            </Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Separator />
          </Steps.Item>
          <Steps.Item index={2}>
            <Steps.Trigger>
              <Steps.Indicator>
                <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
              </Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Title>Review</Steps.Title>
            </Steps.Trigger>
          </Steps.Item>
        </Steps.List>
      </Steps.Root>
    </Stack>

    <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
      <Text fontWeight="600" fontSize="350">Small (sm, default) - 32px indicator</Text>
      <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3} size="sm">
        <Steps.List>
```

### Orientation

Choose between horizontal and vertical layouts.

#### Horizontal

Default orientation, best for wide layouts and wizard headers.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3} orientation="horizontal">
    <Steps.List>
      <Steps.Item index={0}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Box>
            <Steps.Title>Account</Steps.Title>
            <Steps.Description>Create your account</Steps.Description>
          </Box>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={1}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Box>
            <Steps.Title>Profile</Steps.Title>
            <Steps.Description>Complete your profile</Steps.Description>
          </Box>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={2}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Box>
            <Steps.Title>Review</Steps.Title>
            <Steps.Description>Review and submit</Steps.Description>
          </Box>
        </Steps.Trigger>
      </Steps.Item>
    </Steps.List>
  </Steps.Root>
)
```

#### Vertical

Best for sidebars, narrow containers, or when steps need longer descriptions.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3} orientation="vertical">
    <Steps.List>
      <Steps.Item index={0}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Box>
            <Steps.Title>Account Details</Steps.Title>
            <Steps.Description>Set up your email and password</Steps.Description>
          </Box>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={1}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Box>
            <Steps.Title>Personal Information</Steps.Title>
            <Steps.Description>Enter your name and contact details</Steps.Description>
          </Box>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={2}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Box>
            <Steps.Title>Confirmation</Steps.Title>
            <Steps.Description>Review and complete your setup</Steps.Description>
          </Box>
        </Steps.Trigger>
      </Steps.Item>
    </Steps.List>
  </Steps.Root>
)
```

### Indicator content

The `Steps.Status` component renders different content based on step state,
allowing you to customize what appears for complete and incomplete steps.

#### Numbers with checkmarks

Show step numbers that transform into checkmarks when complete.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3}>
    <Steps.List>
      <Steps.Item index={0}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Complete</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={1}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Current</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={2}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Incomplete</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
      </Steps.Item>
    </Steps.List>
  </Steps.Root>
)
```

#### Custom icons

Display semantic icons that change to checkmarks when complete, providing visual
context for each step's purpose.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3}>
    <Steps.List>
      <Steps.Item index={0}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Icons.Person />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Account</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={1}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Icons.Settings />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Settings</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={2}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Icons.Verified />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Verify</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
      </Steps.Item>
    </Steps.List>
  </Steps.Root>
)
```

### Step states

Each step visually indicates its state based on the current progress. Use the
built-in navigation triggers for seamless step management.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={4}>
    <Steps.List>
      <Steps.Item index={0}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Step 1</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={1}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Step 2</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={2}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Step 3</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={3}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Step 4</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
      </Steps.Item>
    </Steps.List>

    <Stack direction="row" gap="400" mt="600">
      <Steps.PrevTrigger asChild>
        <Button variant="outline" size="sm">Previous</Button>
      </Steps.PrevTrigger>
      <Steps.NextTrigger asChild>
        <Button variant="solid" size="sm">Next</Button>
      </Steps.NextTrigger>
    </Stack>
  </Steps.Root>
)
```

### Color palette

Customize the visual theme using the `colorPalette` prop.

```jsx-live
const App = () => {
  const renderSteps = (colorPalette) => (
    <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3} colorPalette={colorPalette}>
      <Steps.List>
        <Steps.Item index={0}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Step 1</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Separator />
        </Steps.Item>
        <Steps.Item index={1}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Step 2</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Separator />
        </Steps.Item>
        <Steps.Item index={2}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Step 3</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
        </Steps.Item>
      </Steps.List>
    </Steps.Root>
  );

  return (
    <Stack direction="column" gap="600">
      <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
        <Text fontWeight="600">primary (default)</Text>
        {renderSteps("primary")}
      </Stack>
      <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
        <Text fontWeight="600">positive</Text>
        {renderSteps("positive")}
      </Stack>
      <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
        <Text fontWeight="600">critical</Text>
        {renderSteps("critical")}
      </Stack>
    </Stack>
  );
}
```

## Guidelines

Use the Steps component strategically to enhance user workflow.

### Best practices

- **Keep labels concise**: Use short, clear step labels that describe the action
  or content
- **Limit step count**: Avoid more than 5-7 steps to prevent overwhelming users
- **Provide descriptions**: Use descriptions for complex steps that need
  additional context
- **Maintain consistency**: Use the same indicator pattern throughout a flow

> [!TIP]\
> When to use
>
> - **Multi-step forms**: Registration, checkout, surveys
> - **Wizards**: Setup flows, configuration, onboarding
> - **Progress tracking**: Task completion, status indicators

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use
>
> - **Linear navigation**: Use tabs or pagination instead
> - **Single actions**: Steps are meant for multi-step processes
> - **Unknown step count**: Use progress bars for indeterminate progress

## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

### Accessibility standards

- **Semantic structure**: Uses tablist semantics (`role="tablist"` and
  `role="tab"`) for proper screen reader navigation
- **Current step indication**: Uses `aria-selected="true"` to announce the
  active step
- **Panel association**: Triggers reference their content panels via
  `aria-controls`
- **Orientation**: Communicated via `aria-orientation` attribute
- **Keyboard navigation**: Tab moves focus between triggers; Enter/Click
  activates a trigger
- **Color independence**: State is communicated through both color AND visual
  indicators (checkmarks, styling) to support users with color vision deficiency

```jsx-live
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Incomplete</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
      </Steps.Item>
    </Steps.List>
  </Steps.Root>
)
```

#### Custom icons

Display semantic icons that change to checkmarks when complete, providing visual
context for each step's purpose.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3}>
    <Steps.List>
      <Steps.Item index={0}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Icons.Person />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Account</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={1}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Icons.Settings />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Settings</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={2}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Icons.Verified />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Verify</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
      </Steps.Item>
    </Steps.List>
  </Steps.Root>
)
```

### Step states

Each step visually indicates its state based on the current progress. Use the
built-in navigation triggers for seamless step management.

```jsx-live
const App = () => (
  <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={4}>
    <Steps.List>
      <Steps.Item index={0}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Step 1</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={1}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Step 2</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={2}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Step 3</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
        <Steps.Separator />
      </Steps.Item>
      <Steps.Item index={3}>
        <Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
          </Steps.Indicator>
          <Steps.Title>Step 4</Steps.Title>
        </Steps.Trigger>
      </Steps.Item>
    </Steps.List>

    <Stack direction="row" gap="400" mt="600">
      <Steps.PrevTrigger asChild>
        <Button variant="outline" size="sm">Previous</Button>
      </Steps.PrevTrigger>
      <Steps.NextTrigger asChild>
        <Button variant="solid" size="sm">Next</Button>
      </Steps.NextTrigger>
    </Stack>
  </Steps.Root>
)
```

### Color palette

Customize the visual theme using the `colorPalette` prop.

```jsx-live
const App = () => {
  const renderSteps = (colorPalette) => (
    <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3} colorPalette={colorPalette}>
      <Steps.List>
        <Steps.Item index={0}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Step 1</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Separator />
        </Steps.Item>
        <Steps.Item index={1}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Step 2</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Separator />
        </Steps.Item>
        <Steps.Item index={2}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status complete={<Icons.Check />} incomplete={<Steps.Number />} />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Step 3</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
        </Steps.Item>
      </Steps.List>
    </Steps.Root>
  );

  return (
    <Stack direction="column" gap="600">
      <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
        <Text fontWeight="600">primary (default)</Text>
        {renderSteps("primary")}
      </Stack>
      <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
        <Text fontWeight="600">positive</Text>
        {renderSteps("positive")}
      </Stack>
      <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
        <Text fontWeight="600">critical</Text>
        {renderSteps("critical")}
      </Stack>
    </Stack>
  );
}
```

## Guidelines

Use the Steps component strategically to enhance user workflow.

### Best practices

- **Keep labels concise**: Use short, clear step labels that describe the action
  or content
- **Limit step count**: Avoid more than 5-7 steps to prevent overwhelming users
- **Provide descriptions**: Use descriptions for complex steps that need
  additional context
- **Maintain consistency**: Use the same indicator pattern throughout a flow

> [!TIP]\
> When to use
>
> - **Multi-step forms**: Registration, checkout, surveys
> - **Wizards**: Setup flows, configuration, onboarding
> - **Progress tracking**: Task completion, status indicators

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use
>
> - **Linear navigation**: Use tabs or pagination instead
> - **Single actions**: Steps are meant for multi-step processes
> - **Unknown step count**: Use progress bars for indeterminate progress

## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

### Accessibility standards

- **Semantic structure**: Uses tablist semantics (`role="tablist"` and
  `role="tab"`) for proper screen reader navigation
- **Current step indication**: Uses `aria-selected="true"` to announce the
  active step
- **Panel association**: Triggers reference their content panels via
  `aria-controls`
- **Orientation**: Communicated via `aria-orientation` attribute
- **Keyboard navigation**: Tab moves focus between triggers; Enter/Click
  activates a trigger
- **Color independence**: State is communicated through both color AND visual
  indicators (checkmarks, styling) to support users with color vision deficiency

## Getting started

### Import

Import the Steps compound component and its TypeScript types:

```tsx
import { Steps, type StepsRootProps } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
```

### Basic usage

The Steps component is a compound component with multiple parts that work together. At
minimum, you need `Steps.Root`, `Steps.List`, `Steps.Item`, `Steps.Trigger`, and `Steps.Indicator`:

```jsx-live-dev
const App = () => {
  return (
    <Steps.Root defaultStep={1} count={3}>
      <Steps.List>
        <Steps.Item index={0}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status
                complete={<Icons.Check />}
                incomplete={<Steps.Number />}
              />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Account</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Separator />
        </Steps.Item>

        <Steps.Item index={1}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status
                complete={<Icons.Check />}
                incomplete={<Steps.Number />}
              />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Profile</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Separator />
        </Steps.Item>

        <Steps.Item index={2}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status
                complete={<Icons.Check />}
                incomplete={<Steps.Number />}
              />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Review</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
        </Steps.Item>
      </Steps.List>
    </Steps.Root>
  );
};
```

## Usage examples

### Controlled vs Uncontrolled

The Steps component supports both controlled and uncontrolled usage patterns:

**Uncontrolled** - The component manages state internally using `defaultStep`:

```tsx
<Steps.Root defaultStep={0} count={3}>...</Steps.Root>
```

**Controlled** - You manage state externally using `step` and `onStepChange`:

```jsx-live-dev
const App = () => {
  const [currentStep, setCurrentStep] = React.useState(0);

  return (
    <Stack direction="column" gap="600">
      <Steps.Root
        step={currentStep}
        onStepChange={(details) => setCurrentStep(details.step)}
        count={3}
      >
        <Steps.List>
          <Steps.Item index={0}>
            <Steps.Trigger>
              <Steps.Indicator>
                <Steps.Status
                  complete={<Icons.Check />}
                  incomplete={<Steps.Number />}
                />
              </Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Title>Account</Steps.Title>
            </Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Separator />
          </Steps.Item>

          <Steps.Item index={1}>
            <Steps.Trigger>
              <Steps.Indicator>
                <Steps.Status
                  complete={<Icons.Check />}
                  incomplete={<Steps.Number />}
                />
              </Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Title>Profile</Steps.Title>
            </Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Separator />
          </Steps.Item>

          <Steps.Item index={2}>
            <Steps.Trigger>
              <Steps.Indicator>
                <Steps.Status
                  complete={<Icons.Check />}
                  incomplete={<Steps.Number />}
                />
              </Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Title>Review</Steps.Title>
            </Steps.Trigger>
          </Steps.Item>
        </Steps.List>
      </Steps.Root>

      <Text color="neutral.11" fontSize="350">
        Current step: {currentStep}
      </Text>
    </Stack>
  );
};
```

### Size variants

The Steps component supports three size variants: `xs` (extra small), `sm` (small, default), and `md` (medium). The size affects the indicator dimensions and typography:

```jsx-live-dev
const App = () => {
  const renderSteps = (size) => (

                incomplete={<Steps.Number />}
              />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Account</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Separator />
        </Steps.Item>

        <Steps.Item index={1}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status
                complete={<Icons.Check />}
                incomplete={<Steps.Number />}
              />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Profile</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
          <Steps.Separator />
        </Steps.Item>

        <Steps.Item index={2}>
          <Steps.Trigger>
            <Steps.Indicator>
              <Steps.Status
                complete={<Icons.Check />}
                incomplete={<Steps.Number />}
              />
            </Steps.Indicator>
            <Steps.Title>Review</Steps.Title>
          </Steps.Trigger>
        </Steps.Item>
      </Steps.List>
    </Steps.Root>
  );

  return (
    <Stack direction="column" gap="800">
      <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
        <Text fontWeight="600">Extra Small (xs) - 24px indicator</Text>
        {renderSteps("xs")}
      </Stack>

      <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
        <Text fontWeight="600">Small (sm, default) - 32px indicator</Text>
        {renderSteps("sm")}
      </Stack>

      <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
        <Text fontWeight="600">Medium (md) - 40px indicator</Text>
        {renderSteps("md")}
      </Stack>
    </Stack>
  );
};
```

```jsx-live-dev
    </Steps.Root>
  );
};
```

## Component structure

The Steps component is composed of the following parts:

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `Steps.Root` | Container that manages step state and provides context |
| `Steps.List` | Container for grouping step items |
| `Steps.Item` | Container for a single step (trigger + separator) |
| `Steps.Trigger` | Clickable element within each step for direct navigation |
| `Steps.Indicator` | Visual marker showing step status |
| `Steps.Number` | Displays the step number (1-indexed) |
| `Steps.Status` | Renders different content based on step state |
| `Steps.Title` | Displays the step title |
| `Steps.Description` | Displays optional hint text |
| `Steps.Separator` | Visual line connecting step indicators |
| `Steps.Content` | Content container that auto-shows/hides based on current step |
| `Steps.CompletedContent` | Content displayed when all steps are complete |
| `Steps.PrevTrigger` | Navigation button to go to previous step |
| `Steps.NextTrigger` | Navigation button to go to next step |
| `Steps.Context` | Render prop for accessing step state |
| `Steps.ItemContext` | Render prop for accessing current item state |

## Accessibility

The Steps component implements accessibility features following WAI-ARIA patterns:

**Roles:**

- `Steps.List` has `role="tablist"`
- Each `Steps.Trigger` has `role="tab"`
- `Steps.Content` panels have `role="tabpanel"`

**State communication:**

- Current step trigger has `aria-selected="true"`
- Triggers reference their content panels via `aria-controls`
- Orientation is communicated via `aria-orientation`

**Keyboard navigation:**

- Tab moves focus between triggers
- Click or Enter activates a trigger (unless `linear` mode restricts it)
- Arrow keys can navigate between triggers

If your use case requires tracking and analytics for this component, it is good practice to add a **persistent**, **unique** id to the component:

```tsx
const PERSISTENT_ID = "checkout-progress-steps";
```

## Testing your implementation

These examples demonstrate how to test your implementation when using Steps within your application. As the component's internal functionality is already tested by Nimbus, these patterns help you verify your integration and application-specific logic.

### Basic Rendering Tests

Verify the component renders with correct structure

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import { Check } from "@commercetools/nimbus-icons";
import { Steps, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus";

describe("Steps - Basic rendering", () => {
  it("renders a tablist with tab items", () => {
    renderSteps();

    // Chakra/Ark Steps uses tablist pattern
    expect(screen.getByRole("tablist")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getAllByRole("tab")).toHaveLength(3);
  });

  it("renders step titles", () => {
    renderSteps();

    expect(screen.getByText("Account")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Profile")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Review")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it("renders step descriptions", () => {
    renderSteps();

    expect(screen.getByText("Set up account")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Complete profile")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("Confirm details")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
});
```

### Step State Tests

Verify state attributes reflect progress

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import { Check } from "@commercetools/nimbus-icons";
import { Steps, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus";

describe("Steps - Step states", () => {
  it("assigns data-state based on step index", () => {
    const { container } = renderSteps();

    // Query triggers which have the data-state attribute
    // In Ark Steps: "open" = current step, "closed" = non-current steps
    // Completed steps are identified by data-complete attribute
    const triggers = container.querySelectorAll('[data-part="trigger"]');
    expect(triggers[0]).toHaveAttribute("data-state", "closed"); // past (completed) step
    expect(triggers[0]).toHaveAttribute("data-complete", ""); // marked as complete
    expect(triggers[1]).toHaveAttribute("data-state", "open"); // current step
    expect(triggers[2]).toHaveAttribute("data-state", "closed"); // future (incomplete) step
    expect(triggers[2]).not.toHaveAttribute("data-complete"); // not complete
  });

  it("shows current step content via tabpanel", () => {
    renderSteps();

    // Step 1 (index 1) is current, so its content should be visible
    expect(screen.getByText("Profile content")).toBeInTheDocument();
    // Other content should not be visible (Ark handles this)
  });
});
```

### Indicator Content Tests

Validate indicator content based on step state

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import { Check } from "@commercetools/nimbus-icons";
import { Steps, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus";

describe("Steps - Indicator content", () => {
  it("renders step numbers for incomplete steps", () => {
    renderSteps();

    // Step 2 (index 1 - current) should show "2"
    expect(screen.getByText("2")).toBeInTheDocument();
    // Step 3 (index 2 - incomplete) should show "3"
    expect(screen.getByText("3")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it("renders complete icon for completed steps", () => {
    renderSteps();

    // Step 1 (index 0) is complete, should show check icon
    expect(screen.getByTestId("complete-icon")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it("renders custom icons when provided", () => {
    render(
      <NimbusProvider>
        <Steps.Root step={0} count={2}>
          <Steps.List>
            <Steps.Item index={0}>
              <Steps.Trigger>
                <Steps.Indicator>
                  <span data-testid="custom-icon">*</span>
                </Steps.Indicator>
                <Steps.Title>Account</Steps.Title>
              </Steps.Trigger>
              <Steps.Separator />
            </Steps.Item>

            <Steps.Item index={1}>
              <Steps.Trigger>
                <Steps.Indicator>
                  <Steps.Number />
                </Steps.Indicator>
                <Steps.Title>Review</Steps.Title>
              </Steps.Trigger>
            </Steps.Item>
          </Steps.List>
        </Steps.Root>
      </NimbusProvider>
    );

    expect(screen.getByTestId("custom-icon")).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
});
```

### Orientation Tests

Verify orientation attribute propagates correctly

```tsx
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/react";
import { Check } from "@commercetools/nimbus-icons";
import { Steps, NimbusProvider } from "@commercetools/nimbus";

describe("Steps - Orientation", () => {
  it("passes orientation to component elements", () => {
    const { container } = renderSteps({ orientation: "vertical" });

    // Check that the tablist has vertical orientation
    const tablist = screen.getByRole("tablist");
    expect(tablist).toHaveAttribute("aria-orientation", "vertical");

    // Separators should also have vertical orientation
    const separators = container.querySelectorAll('[data-slot="separator"]');
    expect(separators.length).toBe(2);
    separators.forEach((separator) => {
      expect(separator).toHaveAttribute("data-orientation", "vertical");
    });
  });

  it("defaults to horizontal orientation", () => {
    renderSteps();

    const tablist = screen.getByRole("tablist");
    expect(tablist).toHaveAttribute("aria-orientation", "horizontal");
  });
});
```
